detail: Cycle 8 real-data v2; HYPOTHESIS.md pre-written (revised by Cycle 7); precondition-gated affine GRU, curved-feature PLE, deficit-aware
Synthetic affine-GRU PoC: precondition gate (GRU vs tabular + shuffle) + deficit-aware curved-count PLE vs log-adequate amount

Precondition gate PASS (GRU-log 0.465 > tabular 0.391; shuffle drop +0.191 = uses order). BUT PLE arms lose badly in the GRU: ple_count-log -0.192, deficit-corrected -0.058 (negative). The PLE-in-GRU deficit (12 correlated bins/step into the recurrence, under-resourced: 25 epochs, no reg/early-stop) is far larger than Cycle 7's static ~-0.04 and dominates the curvature signal - exactly cycle 6's 'PLE training-sensitive' + cycle 7's structural deficit compounding. Step 6 must control it heavily.

User correction: retrain GRU under Cycle-6 regime (minibatch bs256 + per-epoch reshuffle, 30 epochs, val split, early-stop patience 6, best-state restore). The prior full-batch 25-step loop undertrained the wide PLE path (12 bins/feat) and produced a spurious -0.192 deficit.
Step 2 README + intent review: no bugs; flagged dominant monotone-by-rank count feature as the reason curvature reads ~0 (power concern for the deficit-aware estimand, carried into debate).

Debate mode Stage A.1: dispatch ml-critic (Mode 1) on HYPOTHESIS.md, curvature_seq_poc.py, README.md. Primary tension seeded: is the deficit-aware curvature estimand powered to detect a real benefit given the ~0 PoC signal?

ml-critic R1: 4 FATAL (F1 no positive-control/power, F2 PR-AUC rank-invariance masks representation, F3 weak recency-agnostic tabular baseline, F4 amount deficit-reference contaminated), 2 MATERIAL (F5 dense arm drift from HYPOTHESIS, F6 single-feature/independent deficits vs correlated real features). No leakage/code bugs found.
ml-defender R1: CONCEDE F1/F3/F4 (all remain sev 7), CONCEDE F5 (sev 4), REBUT-DESIGN F2 (7->5 via Cycle-6 precedent), DEFER F6. Defender self-verdict critique_wins.

Debate converged: 3 conceded FATAL findings (F1 no positive-control/power, F3 weak tabular baseline, F4 contaminated deficit reference) at adjusted severity 7 force critique_wins (defense_wins unavailable by constitutional constraint). Stage B cannot alter outcome; no rebuttals to challenge. Remediation list becomes the Gate-1 experiment plan.

User approved Gate-1 remediation plan: build hardened PoC addressing F1 (power/positive-control sweep), F3 (strong EWMA+GBM tabular baseline w/ CI), F4 (clean marginal-matched log-adequate deficit reference), F5 (dense arm), F2 (log-loss/Brier), F6 (single-feature scope). No real data until this passes.

Hardened PoC (F1-F6). Power curve FLAT-NEGATIVE (benefit -0.004->-0.041->-0.042->-0.036, never crosses 0). Gate PASS vs strong GBM+EWMA baseline (margin +0.056 CI-clear; shuffle drop +0.236). Oracle ceiling 0.628; log-GRU 0.596 (gap +0.032 CI-clear = real but small headroom). dense-log +0.004 (no free-nonlinearity lever). ple_count HURTS -0.041; log/raw/ple_ref/dense all cluster 0.596-0.600.

Corrected interpretation: NOT estimand-blind. dense (free per-step nonlinearity, a superset of PLE's per-step expressivity) also yields ~0 (+0.004), and the ~0.032 oracle gap is closed by NOTHING (dense/raw/ple all cluster). So no per-step-value lever exists in a GRU: its gate nonlinearities already absorb monotone value-curvature; PLE only imports its deficit (worse as the feature matters more). Cycle 7's static-affine-read curvature lever does NOT transfer to a GRU. Hypothesis REFUTED for the sequence model; Step 6 real-data monotone-curvature targeting NOT justified.

CONCLUSIONS.md + REPORT_ADDENDUM.md: Cycle 8 REFUTED for the GRU. Unified theory (static-vs-recurrent x monotone-curved-vs-non-monotone 2x2). Deployment rule: PLE-encode only non-monotone/band features; leave monotone (linear or curved) on log scalar. Standing follow-up = Cycle 6's real-data non-monotone A/B, now equipped with Cycle 8's validated precondition gate. No Cycle 9 (Cycle 6 already settled non-monotonicity at L=300).

Cycle 8 complete (conclusions_only). Hypothesis refuted for the sequence model with mechanism (GRU gates provide effective per-step nonlinearity; dense-arm null + un-closable oracle gap confirm no per-step lever). Instrument (precondition gate) validated and reusable.
